entry.title: Re: Custom Form with Custom Data Source in SAP Public Cloud (Grow with SAP)

entry.title: Re: Decision Logic Modernization in SAP Integrations: Using RPT-1 for Intelligent Routing Decisions

Hi @arunmaarirajha_kv ,

 

well done. I am highly interested and implementing myself these kind of iflows. Will you share the iflow with us ?

 

Kind regards
Jwan S.

entry.title: Re: Deep Dive into SAP Datasphere Object Store of BDC: Benefits, Architecture and implementation
entry.content:
@mdelibasic

there are missing considerations: whenever you write something into a local table file via replication flow, you need the merge task, the merge task rewrite the entire table into another file/files and keep the old data for at least 7 days.

So 1TB of object store is consumed by the older version of the data, if you load the data once a day, you effectively have 1TB / 7 = 142,8GB. => 0.525 CU / GB.

Unfortunately the storage component is the smallest component in terms of costs, we found that for a table with 150M rows (1.5GB), the costs of only keep updated the data in the object store, extracting it from the source system is 6x higher than keeping it in memory (without transformation successive data read (SQL on file)). the big part of the cost is from Compute and in some cases (fragmentation) from API call.
We tested partitions / delta loading, deferred merge, and changing apache spark application level, and many options (vaccuum / optimize), nothing reduce the costs effectively. 

it seems related to the size of the apache spark cluster that is instantiated at runtime (too big).

I really appreciate if other people try to measure capacity units consumption in "System Monitor -> Capacities" and found different results.

entry.title: Re: EWM Outbound delivery - how to update a custom status...
entry.content: yes, it's not ideal.... but I didn't manage anything else. The API /SCWM/IF_API_WHR_OUTBOUND should support updating custom status in my opinion.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP subaccount D81 credentials are not working
entry.content: I am having this same problem.

entry.title: Re: Unable to monitor Processes/Workflows in SAP BPA
entry.content: Yes, it was just a temporary issue and worked again the next day.

entry.title: Re: Delivery Locked on Auto Invoice via BAPI
entry.content:
The most stable solution is to decouple invoice creation from the immediate posting. Configure your output type to trigger invoice creation after commit (via update task or scheduled output processing). This way:

  • Delivery posting completes successfully.

  • Lock is released.

  • Invoice creation runs without collision.

  • Document flow updates correctly.

entry.title: Re: Understanding Joule - (Step #8) Joule for Consultants
entry.content: Highly informative!!

entry.title: Re: Prevent negative balance in Investment WBS – SAP S/4H...
entry.content: Good day Chris, Thank you for your detailed explanation and recommendations. I have already tested this and we are indeed reaching the same conclusion regarding the behaviour of non event-based settlement and the need for either event-based settlement or an immediate re-settlement after reversals.Thank you for your support and for the helpful references. Best regards, Álvaro

entry.title: Welcome to the Discussion Area!
entry.content:
This forum is your space to connect with other learners, exchange ideas, and deepen your understanding throughout your learning and certification journey.

Here, you can:

  • Raise questions about courses, learning paths, or certification topics

  • Share your own study experiences, tips, and preparation methods

  • Discuss practical applications of what you’ve learned in real project situations

  • Learn from the insights and best practices of others in the community

Whether you are just beginning or already preparing for an certification, this area is meant to help you learn from one another and grow together. We invite you to participate actively, contribute your thoughts, and support fellow learners. We look forward to your discussions and questions!

entry.title: Re: Unable to add New EWM warehouse number to New Busines...
entry.content:
Dear @kkumara,

In Public Cloud embedded EWM the Default Party Entitled to Dispose (PED) in CBC ID 102657 Set Warehouse Address is not an arbitrary Business Partner. The system only allows the BP that represents the plant maintained as Customer No. Plant for the plant(s) assigned to the warehouse. If you enter a regular new BP even a customer/supplier it is rejected with error so maintain Customer No. Plant for the relevant plant in ID 101926 Define Shipping Data for Plants then return to CBC ID 102657 Set Warehouse Address and select that plant-representing BP as Dflt Pty Entld

More details in SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3622534

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Fixed amounts missing in 0CO_OM_CCA_1_D (Cost and Allocation) for target costs
entry.content: @Debbie219Adams, thank you. Do you know which extractor populates the fixed amounts (on total level)?

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP Classical Workflow: A complete guide (Part- 2)
entry.content: thank you for detailed blog

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Amount Calculation in FWZE Despite Correct...
entry.content: Go to FWZZ → Condition tab and check the Price per (unit) field. Ensure it’s set to 1 if you want a direct multiplication. Verify the quotation type (direct vs. indirect) in the currency pair customizing. If necessary, adjust the condition type calculation rule in customizing so the system interprets the rate correctly.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – New software delivery option “Add-On Product”
entry.content:
Hello @Atul_Joshi85 ,

1. The partner has complete freedom of choice, and there is no dependency on SAP BTP ABAP environment releases. In contrast to the classic ABAP approach in on-premise where the add-on is tightly coupled to the underlying ABAP platform release, that's different with ABAP Cloud approach and the SAP BTP ABAP Environment: ABAP Cloud enforces the usage of released API in partner add-ons and SAP ensures that these released APIs remain stable. 

2. Only the customer chooses when to import the new version into their system. Therefore, the partner may need to offer and maintain multiple versions. This also means that the partner is responsible for defining the support period. We do not plan a feature that the partner is allowed to push a new version into the customer systems. The deployment is always initiated by the customer.

entry.title: ABAP in Eclipse logon requesting credentials repeatedly
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am experiencing a persistent login loop when trying to use ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse.

The Issue:

When Eclipse triggers the logon prompt, it redirects me to the browser. However, the logon page refuses to sign me in. When I enter my username and password, the page simply refreshes without logging me in.

What I have observed:

  • If I try to log in using my username, I receive the error:
    "We couldn't authenticate you."

  • However, if I log in using my email address with the same password, it works successfully.

  • This setup was working perfectly until today. Previously, I was not prompted for browser login at all.

Steps already taken:

  1. Cleared browser cache and cookies.

  2. Reset SAP password.

  3. Tried different browsers.

Despite these steps, the issue persists in Eclipse. It seems like the ADT logon window is rejecting the credentials or failing the authentication handshake.

Has anyone encountered this recently in ADT?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Taulia to SAP PO changes update
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want to know how the vendor change price communication from Taulia can be updated in Purchase order without affecting net price before approval process. 

Currently our users are using Taulia for vendor communication. In a month there will be around 4000+ PO communication any changes related to the quantity and deliver date are updated in PO confirmation tab with relevant confirmation category. But if there are changes to the price, where this can be update in PO without changing the net price in PO? Business want to validate the change price and approve it before updating the PO price. 

Regards, 

Varma

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the Discussion Area!
entry.content: Yes. This is a good space for both functional and technical discussion.

entry.title: Re: Unable to add New EWM warehouse number to New Busines...
entry.content: Here is the proper link to the note 3622534 - Error message: The system can't determine the party entitled to dispose. /SCWM/MD068 in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: 🇭🇺 Build AI services using CAP and Generative AI Hub (Budapest, Hungary)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we're using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (genAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with the theory behind genAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you'll become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday April 29th, 2026 in Budapest, Hungary 🇭🇺 from 10:00 am - 04:00 pm. The workshop site is the House of Business Bank Center in Budapest. The event is kindly being hosted by Nagarro.

The event will be held in english.

The instructor is the Developer Advocate @KRiedelsheimer .

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome.

Please note that seating is limited - we can only accomodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Planning calendar customizing in public cloud
entry.content: Hello Community, is there any best practice for maintaining planning calendar in public cloud? Assuming a plant will be live on public cloud in the year 2026. From which year should this customizing be maintained?

2. What are the documents generated at the time of good receipt for non valuated project stock.

3. Is non valuated project stock is visible in MBBS report after GR?

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC Cloud (ECC)
entry.content: Hi, we are also facing this issue. Hoping for a solution soon.

entry.title: Using Client Certificate Authentication in SAP CPI HTTP Receiver Adapter
entry.content:
Introduction

Hi friends, in this blog lets see how to configure the HTTP receiver adapter using client certificate auth type and step by step explain how a PKCS#12 (.p12) certificate is stored in the Keystore, which I have explored in my recent Integration flow development.

What is Client Certificate Authentication?

Client certificate authentication, also known as a mutual authentication method / mutual TLS (mTLS), ensures that:

  • The client (SAP CPI) authenticates itself to the server

  • The server also presents its certificate to CPI

  • Both sides trust each other using certificates issued by trusted CAs

Unlike basic or OAuth authentication, client certificate authentication relies on X.509 certificates and private keys, not usernames or tokens.
For more information about client certificate authentication, refer to the link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/client-certificate-authentication-outbound?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=CLOUD 

Files Provided by the Partner

Our 3rd party client (receiver system) has provided 

  • certificate.crt — signed client certificate (after providing the signing request)

  • private_key.key — private key (We need to generate this via OpenSSL if it is a Production tenant)

  • certificate-chain.pem — intermediate/root CA certificates

To establish the connection, all three must be updated in the tenant keystore, since SAP CPI does not accept .key files directly, these files must be converted directly into a PKCS#12 (.p12) format.

Creating the P12 File

The .p12 file contains:

  • Client certificate (eg, certificate.crt)

  • Private key (eg, private_key.key)

  • CA certificate chain (Intermediate + Root certificates) (eg, certificate-chain.pem) 

Using OpenSSL /Command prompt:

Open the command prompt from the same folder in which all these files are stored. 

Enter the command below, and it will ask you to set the password. After you enter the password, it generates the p12 file in the same folder.

openssl pkcs12 -export \
-in certificate.crt \
-inkey private_key.key \
-certfile certificate-chain.pem \
-out client.p12 \
-name "client-cert"


Note: An export password is required and will be used later during upload into CPI.

Uploading the Certificate to SAP CPI Keystore

  1. Log in to SAP Cloud Integration

  2. Go to Monitor → Keystore

  3. Choose Add → Key Pair

  4. Upload client.p12

  5. Enter the export password

  6. Save

The certificate is now securely stored and managed by CPI.

 

Configuring the HTTP Receiver Adapter

In your integration flow:

  1. Add an HTTP Receiver Adapter

  2. Set Authentication to: Client Certificate

  3. In the Credential Name field, select the alias of the uploaded .p12 from the Keystore.

 

Security Best Practices

  • Protect the .p12 password

  • Use separate certificates for PROD and NON-PROD

  • Monitor certificate expiry dates

Conclusion

Client certificate authentication is a secure and reliable option. Using a P12 certificate from the keystore with the HTTP Receiver Adapter keeps authentication simple and secure.

Thank you for reading this blog. I hope this helps you with your integration. When I started this integration development, I uploaded all the keystore documents separately, but it still ended up failing. Also, I couldn’t find any reference blog on how to handle this. So, I want to share this with others. If you feel this is a long procedure (as I do), please share your ideas and feedback in the comments.

entry.title: Google Pub/Sub Adapter in SAP Integration Suite:
entry.content:
Google Pub/Sub Adapter in SAP Integration Suite:

 Google Pub/Sub is a fully managed messaging service that enables asynchronous communication between applications. With SAP Integration Suite, SAP provides a native Google Pub/Sub Adapter that allows seamless publishing and subscribing to events.

What is Google Pub/Sub?

  • As a Publishers we can send messages to a Topic
  • As a Subscribers we can consume/receive messages from a Topic Subscription

We can Also handle Acknowledgement with in google pub/Sub adapter which will be like Acknowledge after Successful Processing, Acknowledge Immediately, and Do not Acknowledge. 

  • Acknowledge after Successful Processing:
    • The message is only acknowledged to Google Pub/Sub after it has been fully and successfully processed by the SAP Integration Suite iFlow.
    • If processing fails, the message will not be acknowledged and will be redelivered by Google Pub/Sub.
  • Acknowledge Immediately:
    • The message is acknowledged as soon as it is received by the adapter, regardless of the subsequent processing outcome in the iFlow.
    • This means the message will not be redelivered even if the integration flow fails later.
  • Do not Acknowledge:
    • The adapter does not send an acknowledgment.
    • This is typically used in specific scenarios where manual acknowledgment or another mechanism is handled externally.

In my case I am creating a sample scenario where I am sending the IDOC Item data to the external service which is a third party application with micro services

  1. At the sender side we are using the IDOC Handler (from a common Iflow) which then routes the data to Process Direct Adapter to pull the Idoc  data to our Iflow.
  2. In the second step we are using the content modifier Set SAP Message Headers and setting the Header Parameters

  1. As we have few fields to be mapped to the target fields, we need to add a Message mapping step and map the IDOC fields to the target structure
  2. Convert the mapped xml to the equivalent JSON as the target application is accepting only JSON format
  3. Within the JSON data we need to add an array which is not being created by default. For which you will have to write a groovy script and add the array

import com.sap.it.script.v2.api.Message

import groovy.json.JsonSlurper

import groovy.json.JsonOutput

 

def Message processData(Message message) {

    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String)

 

    def jsonSlurper = new JsonSlurper()

    def input = jsonSlurper.parseText(body)

 

    // 1. Process ItemPackage: Remove 'element' and make it an array

    if (input.Data?.ItemPackage?.element) {

        // Extract the content of 'element'

        def elementContent = input.Data.ItemPackage.element

        // Reassign ItemPackage as an array containing that content

        input.Data.ItemPackage = [elementContent]

    } else if (input.Data?.ItemPackage) {

        // If ItemPackage exists but 'element' doesn't, just ensure it's an array

        input.Data.ItemPackage = input.Data.ItemPackage instanceof List ?

                                 input.Data.ItemPackage : [input.Data.ItemPackage]

    }

 

    // 2. Process Data: Convert the single occurence of Data into an array

    if (input.Data != null && !(input.Data instanceof List)) {

        input.Data = [input.Data]

    }

 

    // 3. Serialize back to JSON

    message.setBody(JsonOutput.prettyPrint(JsonOutput.toJson(input)))

   

    return message

}

 

  1. Create  a Receiver and connect the Google pub/Sub adapter with the below parameters

In processing tab you need to select the parameters:

 

Parameter 

Description 

Entity  

Select the entity for message processing:  

·        Topics 

·        Subscriptions  

Operation  

Select the operation to be performed.  

·        Publish Message

·        List  

Project ID  

Specify the Google Pub/Sub project ID.  

Topic ID  

Specify the Topic ID created on Google Pub/Sub service 

Operation Type  

Select the type of operation  

·        Basic 

·        Advanced  

Encode Data  

Select the checkbox to encode data to Base64 

Ordering key  

Specify the ordering key for this message.   

Message Attributes  

Specify multiple message attributes as key value pairs.

 Once the Configuration is done you should be able to trigger the IDOC and send the JSON message to the target application

Conclusion: 

  • The Google Pub/Sub adapter in SAP Cloud Integration (CI) enables real-time, event-driven communication between SAP systems and Google Cloud.
  • It allows CPI to seamlessly publish and consume messages at scale, supporting high-performance, responsive, and loosely coupled integrations.

entry.title: Re: VAT Support for EU Cross-Border Movement of Own Stock with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 230
entry.content: In what version of SAP S/4 HANA On Premise is this functionality planned?

entry.title: Re: The 2025 Developer Insights Survey: The Report
entry.content: @rileyrainey One of the questions in the 2026 survey needs a fix:

entry.title: Commitments in Company Code
entry.content:
Good morning,

I am analyzing commitments when performing budget control for cost centers.

The other day, I had a question: when you create the purchase order, that is when the commitment is generated. That much is clear to me.

But what happens when you have the order approval in the workflow? I know that approval allows you to receive that order.

Let's imagine that it is rejected. We would no longer be able to receive the order, and from what I have seen, that commitment is not removed from the reports.

I also tried rejecting that order to see if it would be removed from the report, but it wasn't.

The only way I have found to remove it would be to mark the order position for deletion, in which case it would be removed.

I would like to know if there is a more automatic way to remove the commitment from the report once the order approval has been rejected.

Best regards.

Juan Antonio Moyano

entry.title: Re: Australia Localization for S/4 HANA Private Cloud 202...
entry.content: Go with OTXAU and activate the AU localization scope item via the Simplified Implementation Framework. This ensures your tax codes, procedures, and compliance reports (BAS, PAYG, PTR, TPAR) are consistent and supported by SAP’s delivered content.

entry.title: Re: How to create a value help for custom and released domain fixed values?
entry.content: I really like RAP in general, MVC, decoupled, doing things in the right way, but things that just work in the old dynpro way of working (with no thought at all), like domain values, to me it boggles the mind that its so tricky to solve this. I read the above post, and still a bit unsure of the entire solution - but gonna give it a go, and boggle some more 😉 a good blog!

entry.title: Re: Getting started with the SAP Logistics Management setup
entry.content: Nice summary, thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: Wrong menu translation in custom report only
entry.content:
Try with the program SAPRSEUG 29197 - Regenerate all CUA interfaces - SAP for Me. No idea what's the difference with RSLANG20.

Also, maybe the reason is not that all your custom programs with original language IT, but they use a custom GUI Status being a copy of standard GUI Status without English texts...

If it doesn't work, send a case to SAP Support.

Our experts and peer community are here to support your learning journey.

👉 Please post your questions or comments below.

We look forward to your contributions.

entry.title: Re: Product Division in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Below are couple of options

  • Create one order type for wholesale and one for retail. In wholesale order type, enforce “same division only”. In retail order type, allow mixed divisions. This is the cleanest way to differentiate because AUART is part of the VOV4 key.
  • Assign different item category groups to materials depending on whether they are sold in wholesale or retail. Maintain separate VOV4 entries for each item category group. This works if product sets are clearly segregated by channel, but adds master data maintenance.
  • If you want minimal master data maintenance, go with some custom logic where you can add a rule:
    • If distribution channel = wholesale → check all items belong to the same division, otherwise raise error.
    • If distribution channel = retail → skip the check.
    • This avoids multiplying order types or item category groups and directly enforces the business rule.

entry.title: ِABC Analysis report
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m currently working on ABC Analysis for materials in SAP S/4HANA using t-code MC40, and I’m facing an issue with the results.

The ABC Analysis report is not showing consumption values consistent with MB51 material documents, even though I have maintained the correct date range.

So I have a few questions:

  1. Does ABC Analysis require specific configuration or LIS setup to retrieve accurate consumption data and how to configure it?

  2. From which table or source does the ABC Analysis report read consumption values (MATDOC, LIS, or another source)?

  3. Is there an alternative report or Fiori app that can perform ABC Analysis for multiple materials reliably?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Validation of PO in Q2 notification
entry.content: We are migrating from ECC to S4, We are not migrating closed documents (Purchase order, Production order, sales orders) to S4. When we create Q2 notification, the system validates and gives an error that the Purchase order is not found.  We need to change this error to warning, So that if the document is not avl in ECC it can give a warning and should allow to create the notification without validating.  How can this be done.  If anyone can explain that would be Great!!!

entry.title: Setting the right expectations around AI for code generation and getting past the inherent limits
entry.content:
With over two decades of working on AI, starting with my master's thesis on multi-layer perceptrons, I’m excited about the recent innovations in this area, especially as related to code generation. In this blog post, I want to share my perspective on using AI in a very specific context: coding with AI agents based on transformer models, as these are the primary tools we have at our disposal for this purpose today. I’ll start with why I use AI and then share some limitations of AI models and how to get around these pitfalls. Let’s start with a quick definition…

Generative AI is Only One Form of Artificial Intelligence

There's a lot of buzz around Artificial Intelligence (AI) these days, and it feels like every conversation about technology eventually lands on this topic. However, the term "AI" is often automatically conflated with "Generative AI" (GenAI). It's important to make a quick distinction. AI is the broad field of making machines smart. Machine Learning (ML) is a subset of AI that uses data to train models, and Deep Learning (DL) is a further subset of ML using neural networks. GenAI, which creates new content, is the latest evolution that has captured everyone's imagination.

Why I Use AI

AI is Practical

Large Language Models (LLMs) have provided us with an unprecedented natural language interface. This makes them incredibly powerful for tasks like web search and information retrieval, where a human can quickly verify the results. Coding is a domain where outputs are verifiable, which makes AI agents a significant efficiency booster. They aren't here to replace us, but to augment our abilities.

It's crucial to remember that LLMs have zero semantic understanding. They are masters of syntax, predicting the next word (or token) in a sequence through an auto-regressive process. They don't "understand" code; they predict what code should look like.

AI Adds Value to My Work

From my experience, AI brings tangible benefits to my development workflow. Here’s how:

  • Understanding Codebases: When jumping into a new or complex project, I use AI to get a quick summary of how different parts of the code interact.
  • Project Planning: Through agent orchestration, AI can help outline project structures, suggest potential components, and map out dependencies.
  • Code Documentation: This is a huge one. AI is exceptionally good at generating boilerplate documentation, docstrings, and comments, which frees me up to focus on the more complex descriptions.
  • Enforcing Guidelines: We can use AI to enforce Clean Core principles and other coding guidelines, ensuring that our code remains compliant and release-ready.

My History with AI

My journey with AI didn't start with the recent hype. It goes back to my college days, long before I entered the SAP ecosystem. My master's thesis was on multi-layer perceptrons and scaled conjugate gradient descent methods. Ever since then, I've been independently researching and following the advancements in this fascinating field.

Potential Pitfalls with AI and Take-aways

Differences Between Prompts and Target Language

Natural language is inherently ambiguous, imprecise, and often not concise. Science, and by extension software development, is about making formal, unambiguous statements. The greatest leaps in scientific history, like the formalization of algebra by Leibniz and Newton, came from moving away from rhetorical natural language and towards formal paradigms. The programming languages we use today are a natural evolution of this.

So-called "vibe coding," where one tries to build complex systems using only conversational language, is great for keynotes and simple examples but falls apart for enterprise-grade systems. I've seen attempts to "formalize" prompts to make agents more reliable, but this is a self-defeating exercise. You end up re-inventing a formal programming language, the very thing you were trying to avoid.

Take-away: This is a fundamental problem that won't be solved by simply scaling models. My practical advice is to iteratively update your agent's prompts and instructions as you code. Let the agent update its own instructions when it runs into issues. And most importantly: never commit code without verifying it yourself. Human experience is still an absolute necessity.

Predicting Syntax vs. Understanding Semantics

As mentioned, all modern coding agents are based on a transformer architecture. They predict syntax, token by token, without any semantic intelligence or understanding of programming metaconcepts. They operate on a vast joint probability distribution and can generalize well within that distribution.

However, when faced with an out-of-distribution (OOD) prompt, they "hallucinate." While syntax prediction without semantic understanding is often "good enough" to boost the efficiency of an experienced software engineer, it's irresponsible to leave critical design decisions to these agents without human oversight.

Take-away: Until a new paradigm emerges that gives AI true semantic understanding, human oversight is paramount. Used responsibly, AI is an incredibly rewarding tool that greatly supports our daily work.

Hallucinations (First and Higher Order)

OOD prompts lead to what I call "first-order hallucinations," where the LLM itself can't generalize and essentially makes things up. The answers are unreliable and require verification. Methods to minimize this include training on more data (scaling) or grounding the LLM with symbolic systems like databases or knowledge graphs.

This leads to what I term "higher-order hallucinations." The grounded data source might be perfectly accurate. However, to access it, the agent must translate your natural language prompt into a formal query. If it hallucinates during this translation, it can create an invalid query (getting no data) or a faulty query that retrieves the wrong correct data. The source is clean, but the retrieval is flawed.

Take-away: In the absence of a new paradigm, it's best to use simpler query sequences instead of one complex one. Simplify the toolset available to your agents and bundle related tasks into a single tool to minimize these translation errors.

Non-Determinism in Answers

While the forward-pass of a transformer is deterministic, the token decoding phase is not. This means if you prompt an LLM five times, you might get five different answers. For coding, this is problematic. Even with the temperature parameter set to zero, non-determinism persists.

Take-away: You never know if you've received the "best" possible generated code, and neither does the AI. This makes human verification absolutely critical, always. For mass refactoring, a robust review process is essential. Interestingly, review agents can help here, but the final decision must rest with a human.

Explainability & Verifiability

LLMs are notoriously black boxes. Billions of floating-point numbers don't offer a semantic explanation for why a particular answer was generated. Research into making these models more transparent is still in its infancy. The lack of semantic understanding, paired with non-determinism, makes true explainability incredibly difficult.

Take-away: For now, the only option for users is to wait for new research and paradigms to emerge.

TL;DR

  1. Human oversight is critical until AI gains a true semantic understanding, which it currently lacks.
  2. Use generative AI to speed your coding, but…
  3. Never commit code without verifying it yourself.
  4. Use simpler query sequences instead of one complex one.
  5. Have a robust code-review process with humans making the final decision.

Next Steps

It is irresponsible for any serious software engineer to ignore the current suite of AI tools, despite their shortcomings. Demonizing them is counter-productive. For those who know what they're doing, these tools are massive efficiency boosters. With responsible use and human oversight, they can be successfully integrated into enterprise workflows and offer many opportunities to improve the human-machine interface.

Outlook

We should not believe all the hype or inflate our expectations of what AI can realistically do today. At the same time, we shouldn't refrain from engaging with these tools in a meaningful way. We, the developers, are still the ones with the creativity, innovation, and semantic understanding. Let's use these tools to amplify that.

Happy coding!

What are your thoughts? Share your experiences with AI in the comments below!

entry.title: Re: Bulgaria EUR Transition_Material inventory balance Mi...
entry.content: Hi, We migrated material inventory using the Material Inventory Balance object for plant BG11. The initial stock is created in BG11, but stock in BG10 is still available. Could you please advise how to correct the stock in BG10 in SAP Public Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Enable Predefined Custom Fields in SAP BTP ABAP Environment for RAP Applications
entry.content:
Hey @raphaelmue, 
I hope you're having a great day.
We're currently evaluating the Abap CF environment for creating SaaS Solution using the predefined custom fields for comsumer individuell data / information.
There's one open questions we couldn't figure out:

What are the concrete steps, if one customer needs more predefined fields (or more of one data-type) than we originally defined?
We tried to adjust the predefined field enabling which is c1 released like the tutorial mentions, but that tiggered the atc checks. 

Our assumption based on untested ideas:
It seems that we have to create a new predefined field enabling and deprecate the that the original following this documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/deprecating-development-objects?locale=en-US 
However, since the predefined field-enabling object must have the same name as the CDS view, does that mean we must create an entirely new CDS view as well?
If yes, what happens to the existing view extensions created by previous customers/consumers? Are those extensions automatically migrated, or would they be lost?

Alternatively, does this scenario require creating a completely new API altogether?

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch (06/2026) @Reply
entry.content:
Liebe Stammtischschwestern und -brüder,

Wir treffen uns am Donnerstag, den 11.06., zum SAP Stammtisch. Lasst uns in schönem gemütlichen Ambiente zum Thema SAP und drumherum austauschen.

Wir haben zwei SAP Stammtisch All-Stars als Vortragende mit dabei:

  • tbd

Wir treffen uns im Solar Room der Reply in der Luise-Ullrich-Straße 14. Bitte haltet auch eure Zu- und Absage aktuell, damit wir die richtige Personenzahl für unsere Planung haben.

Wir freuen uns auf Euer kommen und einen regen Austausch!

SAP Stammtisch Orga Team

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Career Development Planning and Mentoring - Discussions
entry.content:
This space is dedicated to your discussions and questions related to SAP SuccessFactors Career Development Planning and Mentoring learning topics.

Here, you can:

❓ Ask questions and seek clarification

💡 Share insights and best practices

🤝 Connect with SAP HCM Learning experts and fellow learners

Our experts and peer community are here to support your learning journey.

👉 Please post your questions or comments below.

We look forward to your contributions.

entry.title: Re: Product Division in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @KrishnarajaBhat,

In Public Cloud one sales order belongs to exactly one Sales Area (Sales Org + Distribution Channel + Division). You can define common distribution channels / common divisions to reduce master data replication but the sales order is still created for a single sales area

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/6f8bc95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html

IMPORTANT: SAP confirms that the division at header level and item level must be the same in a single sales order. The item division is derived from the product material master so if products are maintained with divisions 10/20/30/40 then mixing them in one sales order is technically not possible regardless of whether the order is Retail or Wholesale

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003443055

That is exactly why:

  • maintaining division in the product master enforces the no cross-division in one order behavior for everyone including Retail
  • If the item division derived from the material matches the sales order header division then the system will not restrict mixing based on division for both Wholesale and Retail
  • Common Division 00 does not solve mixing if items still carry different divisions

Option 1: Recommended and cleanest in Public Cloud

Don’t using Division as the product segregation attribute. Use one common division for the sales org so Retail can technically sell all products in one order and keep the 4 product families using a proper attribute such as:

  • Product Hierarchy (recommended) or
  • Material Group / Product Group or
  • a custom field on product

For example enforce the Wholesale rule with a Public Cloud validation in Custom Fields and Logic via Sales Document Check Before Save (SD_SLS_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE) Badi. SAP explicitly supports these Cloud Badi for sales document checks in Public Cloud

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/cloud-badis-to-check-sales-document-and-items/ba-p/14299492

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2820950

Why this is recommended:

  • Meets both requirements exactly Wholesale restricted, Retail flexible
  • Avoids massive product exclusion/listing maintenance
  • Stays aligned with Public Cloud clean core design no attempt to bypass standard division rules

Option 2: Standard but data-heavy

If the customer insists that products must remain in divisions 10/20/30/40 for organizational/reporting reasons then the only standard way to allow a single Retail order is to ensure Retail-sellable items share one division. In practice that means creating Retail versions of materials /separate material numbers assigned to division 00

To reduce the maintenance effort in this scenario, use common distribution channels / common divisions reference sales areas to minimize repeated master-data maintenance across sales areas

Best Regards,

Dawid

Thanks for checking this blog.

You need add the service plan for "CIS" in the Entity Assignments in the Global Account setup selecting the particular subaccount.

 

Let me know if this helps!

 

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud vs. Lightweight ERP for a Small Rental‑Driven Service Company
entry.content:
I’m currently in the middle of an ERP evaluation and would really appreciate some honest feedback from people who’ve seen similar setups in real life.

The company I’m looking at is small (around 25 employees), operates from two locations, and works in the traffic safety / temporary traffic management space. They provide services around construction sites and public space usage and rent out materials like traffic lights, signs and related equipment. All field work is done with their own vehicles and crews.

Right now, my main question is whether SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition is a good fit at all — or whether a lighter ERP (e.g. Odoo or a similar solution with strong rental support) would actually be the more honest recommendation.

What makes this tricky

Operationally, this is not a classic “sell, deliver, invoice” business.

  • Materials are rented out to projects, often for weeks or months
    They need to be clearly visible as available, reserved, in use, returned, sometimes partially.
  • Projects are non‑linear
    They get extended, paused, changed, and rarely follow a clean end‑to‑end flow.
  • Approvals and permits are long‑living states, not one‑time steps
    They create follow‑ups, reminders and work over time.
  • Tasks and reminders are derived from project and approval states, not created manually as standalone to‑dos.
  • The company runs its own fleet and crews and would like to:
    • Plan routes and daily assignments
    • Reflect what actually happens in the field
    • Feed that back into project status and billing

For now, the scale is still manageable — but the goal is to become more structured without over‑engineering.

Where I’m struggling with SAP Cloud ERP

From what I see so far, SAP S/4HANA Cloud feels very solid as a financial and transactional backbone.
But once I look at:

  • rental‑like material usage over time
  • dynamic task creation based on changing states
  • route and field crew planning

I start to wonder how much of this is realistically doable without adding:

  • SAP Field Service Management
  • additional service or asset modules
  • BTP extensions
  • or even third‑party tools

At that point, the solution stack starts to feel quite heavy for a company of this size.

The honest question I’m asking myself

Would it make more sense to recommend a lightweight ERP with strong rental and field service capabilities (like Odoo or a similar solution with rental depth), instead of pushing a cloud ERP that only really works once several extra products are added?

I’m not looking for a “SAP vs. non‑SAP” debate — I’m genuinely trying to understand:

  • What works well in standard SAP S/4HANA Cloud for small, operationally driven companies
  • Where the hard limits are
  • And where you personally would say: this is better handled outside of Cloud ERP

Any real‑world experience, lessons learned, or “don’t do this” advice would be very welcome.
I’m trying to avoid designing something that looks great on slides but creates friction in daily operations.

Thanks a lot in advance — looking forward to learning from your experiences.

entry.title: SAP HCM Payroll Processing & SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll - Discussions
entry.content:
This space is dedicated to your discussions and questions related to SAP HCM Payroll Processing & SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll learning topics.

Here, you can:

❓ Ask questions and seek clarification

💡 Share insights and best practices

🤝 Connect with SAP HCM Learning experts and fellow learners

Our experts and peer community are here to support your learning journey.

👉 Please post your questions or comments below.

We look forward to your contributions.

entry.title: Meet Your HCM Learning Experts
entry.content:
The HCM Learning Group is supported by a team of SAP experts with many years of experience. We thought you might like to get to know them.

 

Tatiana Dymova

I'm a Product Learning Expert for SAP Payroll solutions based near Heidelberg, Germany. I specialize in helping learners master the full portfolio of SAP Payroll offerings, including SAP HCM Payroll for SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll, Payroll Control Center (PCC), and SAP’s latest payroll innovation - SAP SuccessFactors Payroll. My role is twofold: I develop comprehensive learning content and deliver training sessions for global audiences. Outside of payroll, I’m passionate about traveling, discovering new places, attending theatre performances and concerts, and visiting exhibitions whenever I can. Always happy to connect with fellow professionals - let’s learn, innovate, and succeed together!

Jay Garcia

I’m an SAP Learning instructor for more than 10 years, focusing on SuccessFactors Employee Central's core features and functions, based in Manila, Philippines. My role helps me balance my need for solitude with my appreciation for interaction. Creating content for our courses allows me to enjoy some introspection and alone time, while teaching classes brings out my more social side. Lately, I’ve been rediscovering the joy of jigsaw puzzles and Sudoku.

Vicki Moening

Hello! I’m a Product Learning Specialist at SAP SuccessFactors. As an HR Professional, I've leveraged my extensive experience in HCM and consulting services to focus on client enablement and training, ensuring the successful configuration and implementation of systems. I specialize in Learning Management Systems, Compensation, and Variable Pay, offering training and developing curricula tailored to client needs. I'm always eager to learn new things, love traveling and discovering new cultures, and enjoy finding creative and fun ways to express myself.

Jordi Mosegui Thomas

I'm a product learning expert on the SAP SuccessFactors team, based in Spain. On my day-to-day, I deliver training and live sessions for Partners and Customers, to help them adopting SAP SuccessFactors, achieve their certification goals or get their SFX accreditation. I am passionate about the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite, AI capabilities, and my areas of expertise include Performance & Goals, Employee Central Core and Position Management, Talent Intelligence Hub, Platform and Career and Talent Development solution. I am also responsible for the content development of the learning journeys Employee Central Position Management, Performance & Goals and Platform Advanced and Talent Intelligence Hub. When I am not at work, I love travelling and exploring the world whenever I have the opportunity and learn about other cultures.

Kristin Steinweg

I am a highly experienced HR professional with more than 25 years of experience, bringing extensive process knowledge and expertise across all areas of human resources. As a trainer specializing in payroll within the SAP HCM on-premise environment, the SuccessFactors platform, and Employee Central, I deliver training to both beginners and advanced users, presenting complex topics in a clear, engaging, and structured manner. I place strong emphasis not only on explaining how processes are executed within SAP systems, but also on clearly conveying the underlying rationale behind them.

Lara Golden

As a Product Learning Expert at SAP, I specialize in learning content for SAP SuccessFactors Global Benefits, Compensation and Variable Pay. I joined SAP in 2018 following the acquisition of CallidusCloud and have been focused on creating great learning experiences ever since. You can find me at home on California’s Central Coast, where you'll usually find me walking on the beach or playing my guitar.

Alex White

Hello, community! I am a Senior Product Learning Specialist with a focus on SAP SuccessFactors Reporting Solutions, Employee Central, and Onboarding. I also develop learning content for SAP SuccessFactors. Since 2014, I have been committed to delivering high-quality training and learning materials for SAP, and I have over 25 years of experience teaching technical content. I am based in Knoxville, Tennessee, USA. Let’s learn and grow together.

entry.title: Re: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP Platform Automation - February Edition

entry.title: How to send email with attachments through SAP BTP Job Scheduler service
entry.content:
We have created action that the job scheduler triggers to send mail in CAPM and We are receiving email through SAP BTP Job Scheduler service without any attachments and getting success message.

When we attach any files and try to send the email using same action that the job scheduler triggers, we are getting success message and job scheduler log is also success, no code level error but email not received.

Created an application/service (CAP, Node.js)

This service will:

  • Generate or fetch the attachment (PDF, CSV, Excel, etc.) from Object Store on SAP BTP
  • Compose an email
  • Send the email using:
    • SAP BTP Destination (SMTP)
    • External mail provider (SendGrid)
Can SAP Job Scheduler send an attachment?

entry.title: Re: Product Division in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Thanks for the quick response. In S4 HANA Public cloud, custom order type cannot be created. Similarly, creation of item category also restricted.

entry.title: How to filter a personalization attribute within mail creation in SAP Marketing Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP-community,

We have one question or challenge regarding missing filter options of a personalization attribute within mail creation.

Goal: We want to send mail campaigns to customers whose purchased products have a specific warranty date.

For this we replicate equipment data incl. of warranties from Sales Cloud to Marketing Cloud. A CBO (Custom Business Object) was designed to be able to use equipment details of accounts in Marketing campaigns.

Within the MC segmentation we are able to create the target group by filtering for all customers that purchased products in a specific timeframe. This segmentation works so far and – in my opinion – cannot fix our issue because this segmentation is just about targeting the correct customers.


As second step we create the mail content. As personalization attribute we use a table that shows the equipment of the corresponding customer, with two columns: Description and serial ID (which we both get from the CBO).

But here, we do not only get the products which we want to show because of their warranty date, we get every product the customer has purchased.

What we need is kind of filtering possibility for this table or an option that shows which product is affected by a specific warranty date.

Do you have an idea how to realize this?

Thank you and kind regards
Sören

entry.title: Re: custom page format created in SPAD tcode. but its not...
entry.content:
Hi 

Was the custom page format you have created linked to PDF printer which is used for printing adobeforms.

Thanks,

Prakash J.

entry.title: SAP BI 2025: Did You Know? Interactive Elements Optimize Both User Experience and Report Performance
entry.content:
The BI 2025 release introduces new Interactive Report Elements for Web Intelligence, designed first and foremost to enhance the user experience. Beyond the UX improvements, these new elements also bring a notable performance gain to Web Intelligence reports. In this post, we’ll explore what’s changed, why it matters, and how you can take advantage of these enhancements.

A New Era of Mixed Content

With this release, reports now support a powerful combination of classic report elements and brand-new interactive ones. Interactive Elements fall into two main categories:

  • Visualizations – Tables, charts, and more
  • Filters – Multi-select lists, calendars, and beyond

An Enhanced Experience With Hidden Benefits

Placing filter elements directly on the report canvas—right alongside your visualizations—does more than enhance the user experience. There’s a hidden performance benefit waiting to be unlocked.

Report Experience Meets Performance

When you use in-place filter interactive elements and configure them to target only Interactive Elements, something significant happens.

Instead of reloading the entire report page after each filter selection, only the affected elements refresh—specifically, the targets you’ve defined. Depending on your report’s complexity and deployment environment, the performance gains can be substantial.

How to Identify Eligible Elements

How can you distinguish Interactive Elements within the user interface? There’s a simple way: look for the Icon Indicator. Interactive Elements are marked with a small blue square on their representative icons, making them easy to spot at a glance.

For example, in the Document Structure Pane:

 

 

How to Select the Right Targets for Filters

When working in Design Mode, select a Filter Interactive Element to reveal its dedicated settings pane. This pane displays all available targets. Here’s how to identify and verify them:

Eligible Target Indicators – Each eligible target is marked with a small blue square on its icon, making it easy to distinguish from non-eligible elements.

Hover to Highlight – Hover your mouse cursor over a target’s name in the settings pane. The corresponding element will be highlighted on the report canvas with a flashing blue rectangle and a filter icon.

Canvas-Level Preview – Alternatively, hover directly over a Filter Interactive Element on the report canvas. This will instantly highlight all associated targets, providing quick visual confirmation.

The result: Reduced load times, smoother interactions, and a more efficient reporting experience.

How to Verify This Performance Optimization Is Active

Want to confirm your report is benefiting from this enhancement? Follow this simple validation method:

  1. In Design Mode, add a Blank Cell containing the formula =CurrentTime()
  2. Create your Interactive Element visualizations and associated filters
  3. Run the report and interact with an in-place filter
  4. If the timestamp in the Blank Cell remains unchanged, the performance optimization is successfully applied

Key Takeaway

With minimal configuration adjustments, report consumers can now leverage the full capabilities of the Web Intelligence engine—achieving faster response times and significantly improved interactivity.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cross Trace: MOVE_CAST_ERROR in /BOBF/CL_X_TRACE_BUFFER when tracing Fiori App F0798
entry.content: It's fixed by the note 3713450 - Runtime Error when using ABAP Cross Trace for Managed Runtime (BSP Framework) - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Rap: Excel Upload and Download Excel using Rap only
entry.content: @vuong123hihi In case you want to create SAP objects based on Excel input data, the only thing that makes sense is to call EML. Find the TP objects corresponding to PR and PO and call those. Or include middleware like Integration Suite to handle reading the Excel and calling public APIs.

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch (06/2026) @Reply

entry.title: Manage Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Good morning,

I have placed a purchase order where I have added the WBS Element and made the advance payment request and payment for that order.

Due to circumstances, I had to change the WBS for the order, and when I went to edit it, the field could not be modified (I understood that it was not allowed because of the purchase order, since it has the advance payment linked to the order).

So, the option I thought of was to cancel the advance payment and the request, and this allowed me to modify the WBS in the purchase order.

I put the question to the gurus: is there any other way to edit that purchase order without having to cancel the payment and the advance request?

Best regards

Juan Antonio Moyano

entry.title: Re: SAC - Translation of public Dimensions
entry.content: Hi, we have created an influence for this topic, so we attach the link just in case you want to vote. Kind regards. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#idea/367440

entry.title: Re: Please advise if SAP Business One is align with IFRS...
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you checked the How to Guide available:

https://help.sap.com/doc/011000358700000118312011e/8.82/en-US/HowToRevenue_IFRS.pdf

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Cannot integrate SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Looks like the url to token endpoint is not correct as mentioned here: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/netweaver-troubleshooting-for-abap-cloud-based-systems

  • Error: Can't create HTTP Client: Error when setting oauth token

    The value in the field Token Endpoint in the communication system isn't correct. It must be the SAP Cloud ALM service key parameter url followed by /oauth/token.

Best regards,
Thomas

entry.title: Re: Taulia to SAP PO changes update
entry.content: Don’t let Taulia overwrite the PO net price directly. Instead, capture the vendor’s proposed price in a separate condition type or custom confirmation category, and use Flexible Workflow to approve/reject before updating the net price. This aligns with how SAP already handles delivery/quantity changes via confirmation categories.

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Orders
entry.content:
There is no way to edit the WBS in a PO with advance payments without cancelling/reversing those payments first. The two compliant options are either (a) reverse and re‑post, or (b) create a new PO.
If the business case is that the cost should move to a new WBS, you can leave the PO as is and instead transfer the costs in Project System (CJ88 or settlement rules). This way, you don’t touch the PO but still realign costs to the new WBS.

entry.title: Deleting entries from table /SCDL/DB_DF in EWM directly (EWM 9.5 SP8)
entry.content:
Hello all,

I've an requirement to delete entries from table /SCDL/DB_DF directly (i.e. w/ SE16), because it seems that they can't be corrected/adjusted w/ SAP program /SCWM/DLV_CHECKDATA.
Does anybody know, if this can cause inconsistencies or if it can be done w/o any risk?

I don't know  much about SAP EWM, but I found a note (3198881 - Document Flow Inconsistencies In EWM Delivery Orders (Inbound Or Outbound) ), which describes inconsistencies In EWM Delivery Orders, so I don't think it's a good idea to directly delete entries from that table.

Thanks & regards
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Commitments in Company Code
entry.content:
Why rejection does not remove commitments is because, the PO is already created in the system, so the commitment is booked against the cost center/WBS. Workflow approval is a business process control, not a financial posting control. Rejection only prevents follow‑on processes (like goods receipt or invoice), but the PO technically still exists, so the commitment remains visible. That’s why you only see the commitment disappear when you flag the PO item for deletion.

The only standard options are to delete the PO item or reduce its value to zero. If you want commitments to reflect approval status, the best practice is to use Purchase Requisitions with workflow approval before PO creation. Alternatively, you can add custom logic to automate deletion/adjustment on rejection.

entry.title: Issue with Deferred Revenue Posting in SAP ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a question about deferred revenue handling in SAP ByDesign. I’m noticing that when I receive down payments (which I expect to be recorded as deferred revenue), the system is still posting them directly to the sales G/L account. I’m using Bank Payment Advice, but I’m not seeing an option to post to a liability account for deferred revenue. Has anyone encountered this, or do you have any suggestions on how to correctly route it? Once the service is delivered, I want it to post as domestic sales, but I’m not sure what steps I might be missing.

Thanks in advance for any advice!

entry.title: EWM Good Receipt based automatic inbound & HU - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

   Client required for once do Goods Receipt process automatic inbound delivery & handling unit to be created.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error in Year Closing due to AUCs with Net Book Value...
entry.content: I have tried both of the suggested methods and executed AJAB, but the system is still displaying the same error for both AUCs, and the asset year closing is still not successful.

entry.title: Re: EWM Good Receipt based automatic inbound & HU - S/4HA...
entry.content:
For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, in an embedded WM scenario, the standard system behavior is as follows:

Standard Process Overview: 

1. Purchase Order Creation

    • The PO is created in the system (e.g. via Manage Purchase Orders app).

2. Inbound Delivery Creation 

      • In Public Cloud with embedded EWM, the inbound delivery is normally created before Goods Receipt.

      • This can happen:

        • Automatically (depending on configuration and document type) - 3669049 - Automatic inbound delivery creation failure after supplier confirmation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition . Also, please check the APP: Schedule Inbound Delivery Creation" app (F2798)

        • Or manually via Create Inbound Delivery app.

 

3. Goods Receipt in EWM

    • GR is posted against the inbound delivery, not directly against the PO.

    • When GR is posted in EWM:

      • Warehouse tasks are generated (if configured)

      • Handling Units (HUs) can be created automatically depending on:

        • Packing instructions

        • Packing specification

        • Warehouse process type

Automatic Packing in the Inbound Delivery | SAP Help Portal 

Important Clarification

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it is not standard design that:

Posting a backend Goods Receipt directly against the PO automatically creates an inbound delivery and HU afterwards.

Instead:

  • The system expects inbound delivery creation first.
  • EWM processes are delivery-based.
  • HU creation is normally triggered during packing or GR against inbound delivery.

If the requirement is:

Create PO → Post GR (backend) → Automatically create inbound delivery + HU

This is not supported as a standard flow in Public Cloud.

Kind Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP S4 EDI AI features
entry.content:
Is anyone using the S4 AI feature for EDI, would like to learn how it works. Any information and experience shared will be great.  

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Deleting entries from table /SCDL/DB_DF in EWM direct...
entry.content:
Hello @DanielB 

Don't do that! If you have data inconsistencies and if they can't be corrected with the /SCWM/DLV_CHECKDATA report, address the issue to SAP support with an OSS ticket. That's what the support is for.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: ِABC Analysis report
entry.content:
• MC40 reads from LIS (S031), not MATDOC.
  • MB51 reads from MATDOC.
  • To align, either update LIS structures or switch to Fiori apps/CDS views for ABC Analysis.
  • The recommended approach in S/4HANA 2023+ is to use the Perform ABC Analysis app or embedded analytics, not MC40.

entry.title: Re: Retired exam format: C_ABAPD: SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud
entry.content: Now the certification has completely disappeared. Can someone from SAP provide information here?

entry.title: Integration: SAP SuccessFactors with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Integration Architecture

The integration leverages SAP Master Data Integration (MDI) service running on SAP Business Technology Platform as the central hub for data replication between systems .

Key components include:

  • SAP Master Data Integration Service: Acts as the middleware for data transformation and routing
  • SAP Integration Suite: Provides cloud integration capabilities and pre-packaged integration content
  • Communication Scenarios: Enable secure data exchange between systems
  • Web Services and APIs: Support real-time and batch data synchronization

Integration Methods

  1. SAP Delivered Integrations

    • Full end-to-end integration processes for specific hybrid cloud scenarios
    • Pre-configured best practices with guided configuration and onboarding 
    • Scope item JB1 for SuccessFactors Employee Central integration 

  2. Template-based Integrations

    • Simplified integration templates primarily for standard scenarios
    • Leverages SAP Integration Suite with pre-built integration flows 

  3. Customer Driven Integrations

    • Custom integrations using SAP Integration Suite and APIs
    • Supports specific business requirements beyond standard scenarios 

Data Flow Scenarios

Scenario Direction Data Objects Frequency
Employee Master DataSF → S/4HANAPersonnel data, organizational assignmentsReal-time/Scheduled
Organizational StructureSF → S/4HANACompany codes, departments, positionsReal-time/Scheduled
Cost Center DataS/4HANA → SFCost center master dataScheduled
Payroll ResultsSF → S/4HANAPayroll posting documentsAfter payroll run
Availability DataSF → S/4HANAEmployee availability informationReal-time

Configuration Steps

  1. Pre-requisites

    • SAP Business Technology Platform subscription
    • Communication user setup in both systems
    • Required authorizations and certificates 

  2. SuccessFactors Configuration

    • Create communication users for MDI integration
    • Configure OAuth connections for secure authentication
    • Set up business scenarios for data replication 
    • Enable address mapping and field mappings

  3. S/4HANA Public Cloud Configuration

    • Activate communication scenarios (e.g., SAP_COM_0193 for Employee Central integration)
    • Create communication systems and arrangements 
    • Configure destinations for data flow
    • Set up Business Integration Builder for infotype mapping

Key Integration Points

  • Employee Master Data: Replicates personnel information, employment details, and personal data from Employee Central to S/4HANA for finance and reporting purposes 
  • Organizational Structure: Synchronizes company codes, cost centers, departments, and positions to maintain consistent organizational hierarchy
  • Time Management: Integrates time data and availability information for workforce planning and scheduling 
  • Payroll Data: Transfers payroll results from SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll to General Ledger Accounting in S/4HANA 

Technical Considerations

  • Authentication: OAuth 2.0 and certificate-based authentication for secure communication
  • Data Mapping: Field mappings defined in SAP Note 3078790 for New Business Partner Model 
  • Error Handling: Data Replication Monitor in Employee Central provides status tracking and error resolution 
  • Performance: Maximum 2000 line items per payroll posting document 

Monitoring & Maintenance

  • Data Replication Monitor: Available in Employee Central for monitoring replication status and re-running failed jobs 
  • Integration Monitoring: SAP Integration Suite provides comprehensive monitoring capabilities
  • Query Administration: S/4HANA maintains query administration tables for tracking integration requests 
  • Application Logs: Detailed logging for troubleshooting and audit purposes

Best Practices

  • Fit-to-Standard Approach: Limit customizations to accelerate implementation and simplify ongoing maintenance 
  • Master Data Governance: Establish clear data ownership with SuccessFactors as the leading system for employees 
  • Security: Implement proper authentication mechanisms and follow SAP security guidelines
  • Testing: Thoroughly test integration scenarios in development environments before production deployment
  • Documentation: Maintain comprehensive documentation of field mappings and custom configurations

entry.title: Re: Deleting entries from table /SCDL/DB_DF in EWM direct...
entry.content: You should not delete entries directly from /SCDL/DB_DF in EWM. Direct deletion can cause serious inconsistencies in delivery orders and document flows. If the /SCWM/DLV_CHECKDATA report cannot fix the data, the safe approach is to open an OSS ticket with SAP Support so they can resolve it properly without risking system integrity.

entry.title: Employee Central Dependent Workflow optimisation
entry.content:
An employee X submits a addition of a dependent and a workflow is triggered.

The agent verifying the details find out that the attachment is not a relevant one and returns the request to employee to withdraw and submit a new request.

Employee gets a notification with details, and he finds two options on workflow resubmit and withdraw.

Actual observation : The resubmit, submits the request but does not allow any changes to previous data including change of attachment.

Question 1: On dependent can the resubmit provide an option to correct the attachment and resubmit the request with corrected information, if yes please provide the setup details.

Question2 :  is there a possibility to disable the resubmit button on dependent workflow and employee has only option to withdraw and he initiates a new request again with correct information.

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Orders
entry.content: You cannot change the WBS in a purchase order that has advance payments without first canceling or reversing those payments. The safe options are to reverse/repost the payments or create a new PO. Alternatively, you can leave the PO unchanged and move the costs to the new WBS using Project System (CJ88 or settlement rules) without touching the PO.

entry.title: How to display row number and column letters like excel in SAC table?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am creating table formulas in SAC. Is it possible to display row number and column letters like excel in SAC table?

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance!

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: Responce time of ChaRM action
entry.content: Note about "times 5sec": in 2026, the note 1426029 says "After implementing this note, you will be able to set the value in the range from 1 to 9999. That is, the value of "CHECK_NTIMES_SOCM" multiplied by 5 seconds (for export) or 10 seconds (for import) defines the time, the CRM transaction is waiting for the export or import to finish."

entry.title: Re: Error in Year Closing due to AUCs with Net Book Value...
entry.content:
Try this ,

 Tcode:AS02 enter your AUC asset then go to depreciation area tab > double click on the depreciation area 01> Menu bar > extras> depreciation area info and set a check mark on "Negative Values Allowed"

entry.title: Re: ِABC Analysis report
entry.content: could you please recommend an app

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors: Build your Adoption Program​ to Maximize Results (English; APAC)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the ‘Will you be attending?’ option will NOT register you for the event.

To register for this event, please click here: Register Here

SAP SuccessFactors: Build your Adoption Program​ to Maximize Results (English; APAC)

This session will help you understand the relevant components of an adequate adoption program to maximize your cloud investment and ensure sustained business value. We will walk you through the essentials to orchestrate Change Management, Governance, Sustainment, and Value Management and how they work together to drive ongoing adoption, solution optimization and user experience.​ Participants will explore best practices and take away actionable templates to build an initial transformation roadmap, define clear roles and responsibilities, and identify the right executive sponsors and stakeholder groups.​ This webcast is a foundational session that will lead into optional follow‑on topic specific webcasts on your preferred topics.. At the end, you will leave with a concrete structure and toolkit to start or refine your own SuccessFactors adoption program.

Please contact SAP_SuccessFactors_Onboarding@sap.com for any questions on this event.

Here is a link to our Global schedule

how to resolve this error.

APIProxy revision xx of xxxx_v1 does not exist in environment test organization xxxx

Thank you

entry.title: Re: From Manual to Automated: Managing Day-in-Lieu Credits in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Great use case! Turning a manual Day-in-Lieu credit process into an automated solution using Integration Center and MDF is a solid example of smart optimization.

entry.title: Re: eDocument Cockpit Incoming Supllier Invoice Colombia...
entry.content: Did you receive the xml from test portal of the vendor?

entry.title: Re: Issue with reduction of dependent requirements after...
entry.content:
Why the dependent requirement stays at 10 is because, they are pegged to the planned order quantity (10 pcs in your case). When you confirm without yield (i.e., no finished goods reported), the system doesn’t reduce the planned order’s requirement. Even though you consumed 1 pc of the component with a 261 movement, the system interprets this as a withdrawal, not as a reduction of the planned order’s requirement. That’s why in POM1 the withdrawal quantity remains 0 — because the confirmation didn’t report yield.

Requirement reduction in REM is tied to yield reporting (confirmation of finished product). Only when yield is posted does the system reduce the dependent requirements accordingly. Component consumption alone (without yield) doesn’t trigger requirement reduction.

entry.title: Re: Comunidade SAP Joinville em movimento 🌍
entry.content: Muito gratificante ver o ecossistema SAP em Joinville ganhando força! Vamos juntos tornar  essa comunidade um espaço para a troca de experiências que acelera a resolução de problemas reais do dia a dia.

Please update us, thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Planning calendar customizing in public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Hammer1,

A Planning Calendar is mainly relevant when you use periodic lot-sizing for example lot-size procedure PK. In that case the system plans procurement proposals against the planning periods defined in the calendar and SAP explicitly references the scenario planning a material using a planning calendar with lot-size procedure PK

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3525620

SAP also explains that monthly/weekly/planning-calendar/accounting-period requirements are created for the first working day of the period

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3136627

In Public Cloud you maintain it via CBC ID 102629 Define Planning Calendar

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-to-create-planning-calendar-in-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-system/qaq-p/12805637

SAP doesn’t prescribe one universal start year. The practical best practice is:

1. Start early enough for testing and long lead times

Maintain the calendar at least 1 year before go-live so it covers:

  • integration testing / cutover rehearsals
  • early forecasting/PIRs
  • long lead-time materials that may create proposals dated before go-live

For a 2026 go-live a safe baseline is Valid-from = 01.01.2025

2. Keep it far enough into the future for your planning horizon

Keep the calendar valid to cover your operational planning horizon plus buffer. Typical safe baseline = go-live + 5 years so in your case Valid-to 31.12.2031

If you have very long lead times / long-term planning go with +10 years

3. Governance rule so you never hit expiry

Add a simple operating procedure extend the planning calendar once per year many customers do this in Q4, also ensure your factory/holiday calendars are extended far enough because planning periods rely on working days

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing-production-planning/configuring-production-planning-master-data

Best Regards,

Dawid
